title: Purge-aware race-sequence CV before any new feature test
summary: Your current best features are heavily sequential.
rationale: Your current best features are heavily sequential. `GroupKFold` prevents race leakage across groups, while `TimeSeriesSplit` explicitly warns that future observations can leak into past without ordered splits and supports a `gap`. For this task, rows adjacent to actual pit events are especially leak-prone because lag/rolling features can encode stop resets too directly.
implementation_hint: Benchmark every candidate under a stricter splitter: hold out full `Race_Year` groups, sort within each train fold by `Race_Year/Driver/LapNumber`, and add a small purge gap around fold boundaries or around rows immediately after `PitStop=1` when generating lagged features. Keep the model unchanged first; treat CV drop vs LB gain as the signal.
expected_effect: Lower local CV but better public-LB alignment; reduces false positives from overfit sequential features.
risk: May look worse locally and slow iteration if the existing CV was optimistic.
